name: Zaynab al-Sughra
title: Patient Sister
shortDescription: Another daughter of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) who was present in Karbala alongside her sister Zaynab al-Kubra (SA) and bore the heavy burden of captivity.
fullStory:
Zaynab al-Sughra (SA) bint Ali (AS) was another daughter of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS). In Amir al-Mu'minin's household, there were other daughters named Zaynab (Zaynab al-Kubra, Zaynab al-Sughra, etc.). She was also among the ladies who accompanied her brother Imam Hussain (AS) on the journey to Karbala.

This noble lady was shoulder to shoulder with her sister Lady Zaynab al-Kubra (SA) through all stages of the journey from Medina to Mecca and then Karbala. On Ashura, she too shared the heart-wrenching grief of losing her loved ones.

After the martyrdom of Imam Hussain (AS) and the looting of the tents, Zaynab al-Sughra, along with other ladies and young children, was taken captive. The role of these ladies in protecting the life of Imam Sajjad (AS) and delivering sermons to expose the Umayyad regime in Kufa and Sham was unparalleled.

Patience, resilience, and submission to the divine will are the great lessons these ladies taught history by enduring captivity.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى عَقِيلَةِ الطّالِبِيِّينَ، السَّلامُ عَلى المَسْبِيّاتِ المَظْلُوماتِ...
quote: (In Ziyarat:) Peace be upon the wise ladies of Talib, peace be upon the oppressed captive women...
